title: Gingered Jamaican Fettuccine Papillote
description: Savory, crispy Jamaican dish featuring fettuccine, pescatarian-friendly. Inspired by Caribbean flavors and Italian technique.
instructions: Cook fettuccine according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add garlic, red onion, and ginger paste. Cook for 3-4 minutes, until the onion is translucent.
Add allspice, cayenne pepper, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the skillet. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add cooked fettuccine to the skillet, tossing to coat with the spice mixture.
Transfer the fettuccine mixture to a parchment-lined baking dish and top with a sprinkle of jerk seasoning mix.
Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil and bake for 10-12 minutes, until the fettuccine is heated through.
Remove the foil and bake for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the top is lightly browned.
Garnish with fresh thyme and serve hot.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional jerk seasoning mix if desired.
Leftovers can be refrigerated for up to 3 days and reheated in the oven or microwave.
